title: David's Oath of the Horatii Quiz
description: This quiz is for the video David, Oath of the Horatii.
topic_path: David's Oath of the Horatii Quiz

assessment_items.question_content:
**David's *Oath of the Horatii* set the standard for the Neoclassical style in its mannerist formal qualities and whimsical content.**  

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3a405daf361c63f278a53156c050e65f.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **David's *Oath of the Horatii* set the standard for the Neoclassical style in its classical formal qualities and moralizing content.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of David's *Oath of the Horatii*?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3a405daf361c63f278a53156c050e65f.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Although painted in Rome prior to the French Revolution, the work embodies the ideals of moral virtue and patriotism of those who opposed the monarchy.**

choices.content: David's rendering of anatomy and use of lighting suggest the influence of Greco-Roman and Renaissance sculpture that he studied in Rome.
Drawn from Roman history, the story highlights the heroicism of the men who have sworn to defend Rome to the death.
David rejected the poetic excess of the Rococo style in favor of a paired down image and heavily structured composition.
David rejected patriotism and embraced the idea of monarchy by using rigid and upright forms for the male figures.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The story of the *Oath of the Horatii* emphasizes the [[☃ dropdown 1]] of self-sacrifice for the sake of a greater moral cause instead of concern over one's own personal interests.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3a405daf361c63f278a53156c050e65f.png)
hints: **The story emphasizes the heroicism of self-sacrifice for the sake of a greater moral cause instead of concern over one's own personal interests.**

assessment_items.question_content: ***Oath of the Horatii* reflects the Enlightenment idea that [[☃ dropdown 1]] were too emotional and devoted to self and family.**
hints: **The gendered division of the figure groupings reflects the Enlightenment position that women were too emotional and devoted to personal concerns of self and family.**
